name: Loukanika
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Fresh pork rind, boiled for 2 hours, drained, and ground
1 Bay leaf - ground in a mortar
recipeInstructions:
1. Grind the pork, pork rind, and fatback through the fine blade of a meat chopper.
2. Combine in a large bowl with all the seasonings. Knead thoroughly.
3. Store in the refrigerator while preparing the casing.
4. Rinse salted sausage casing (pork intestine) under cold running water several times, ensuring it stays inside a large pan to avoid loss down the drain. Allow to drain on a linen towel.
5. Use a pastry bag to force the stuffing into the casing, pinching at 3-1/2 to 4 inch intervals to allow space for sausage links.
6. Cook by poaching in water for 1 hour, then draining and discarding cooking water. Fry sausages in a frying pan over moderate heat or as suggested in any recipe. Drain and serve hot.
7. Note: Store uncooked sausages in meal-sized batches and freeze. Use within a day or two if not frozen.
